message:
🐛 Remove all rm -rf commands from missions (#1378)

Security audit found 7 files with rm -rf. Fixed or deleted:

- platform-k0s.json: rm -rf ~/.kube/config → k0s reset (official method)
- platform-docker-desktop-k8s.json: rm -rf /Applications/Docker.app → brew uninstall --cask docker
- platform-kubeadm.json: sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube → rm $HOME/.kube/config
- install-porter.json: rm -rf ~/.porter → rm -r ~/.porter (no force flag)
- open-webui-929: DELETED (raw issue dump with rm -rf /mnt/...)
- kpt-962: DELETED (raw issue dump with sudo rm -rf /Library/Developer)

seaweedfs-2445 kept — "rm -Rf" is in a config file comment, not executable.

Policy: rm -rf is NEVER acceptable in mission commands. Use project-specific
uninstall tools (k0s reset, brew uninstall, kubeadm reset) or targeted
file deletion without -f flag.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:

-{
-  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
-  "name": "kpt-962-homebrew-installation-fails-on-macos-11-big-sur",
-  "missionClass": "solution",
-  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
-  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
-  "mission": {
-    "title": "kpt: homebrew installation fails on macOS 11 (Big Sur)",
-    "description": "homebrew installation fails on macOS 11 (Big Sur). This issue affects 22+ users.",
-    "type": "troubleshoot",
-    "status": "completed",
-    "steps": [
-      {
-        "title": "Identify kpt troubleshoot symptoms",
-        "description": "Check for the issue in your kpt deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt -n kpt --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Review kpt configuration",
-        "description": "Inspect the relevant kpt configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt\nkubectl get configmap -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kpt\n```\nI'm running the beta version of macOS 11, and have been running into issue trying to `brew upgrade` to the latest version."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Apply the fix for homebrew installation fails on macOS 11 (Big Sur)",
-        "description": "> Another solution:\n> \n> ```\n> sudo rm -rf /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools\n> sudo xcode-select --install\n> ```\n> \n> Thanks `brew doctor` for this hint.\n\nI had tried that.\nI would imagine it depends on the version of Xcode installed, I didn't have success with 12.2 but I did with the 12.3\n```yaml\n==> Upgrading 1 outdated package:\ngooglecontainertools/kpt/kpt 0.32.0 -> 0.33.0\n==> Upgrading googlecontainertools/kpt/kpt 0.32.0 -> 0.33.0\n==> Downloading https://github.com/GoogleContainerTools/kpt/archive/v0.33.0.tar.\nAlready downloaded: /Users/xander/Library/Caches/Homebrew/downloads/ac1b2a55ace325dc4b88fa6472234f6240ba15cd90ad89237df2812f6c8f5011--kpt-0.33.0.tar.gz\nError: Your CLT does not support macOS 11.0.\nIt is either outdated or was modified.\nPlease update your CLT or delete it if no updates are available.\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Upgrade kpt to include the fix",
-        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kpt:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade kpt kpt/kpt --namespace kpt\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kpt\nhelm list -n kpt\n```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Confirm homebrew installation fails on macOS 11 (Big Sur) is resolved",
-        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt -n kpt --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n kpt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
-      }
-    ],
-    "resolution": {
-      "summary": "> Another solution:\n> \n> ```\n> sudo rm -rf /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools\n> sudo xcode-select --install\n> ```\n> \n> Thanks `brew doctor` for this hint.\n\nI had tried that.\nI would imagine it depends on the version of Xcode installed, I didn't have success with 12.2 but I did with the 12.3 CLT.\n\nNot arguing, just noting the solution may not work for all.",
-      "codeSnippets": [
-        "==> Upgrading 1 outdated package:\ngooglecontainertools/kpt/kpt 0.32.0 -> 0.33.0\n==> Upgrading googlecontainertools/kpt/kpt 0.32.0 -> 0.33.0\n==> Downloading https://github.com/GoogleContainerTools/kpt/archive/v0.33.0.tar.\nAlready downloaded: /Users/xander/Library/Caches/Homebrew/downloads/ac1b2a55ace325dc4b88fa6472234f6240ba15cd90ad89237df2812f6c8f5011--kpt-0.33.0.tar.gz\nError: Your CLT does not support macOS 11.0.\nIt is either outdated or was modified.\nPlease update your CLT or delete it if no updates are available.",
-        "% ls -l /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs\ntotal 0\nlrwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel   15 17 Sep 22:50 MacOSX.sdk -> MacOSX10.15.sdk\ndrwxr-xr-x  8 root  wheel  256 17 Sep 22:50 MacOSX10.15.sdk",
-        "$ softwareupdate --list\nSoftware Update Tool\n\nFinding available software\nSoftware Update found the following new or updated software:\n* Label: Command Line Tools beta 3 for Xcode-12.2\n\tTitle: Command Line Tools beta 3 for Xcode, Version: 12.2, Size: 440919K, Recommended: YES,\n$ softwareupdate -i \"Command Line Tools beta 3 for Xcode-12.2\""
-      ]
-    }
-  },

@@ -1,77 +0,0 @@
-{
-  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
-  "name": "open-webui-929-feat-allow-login-without-authentication",
-  "missionClass": "solution",
-  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
-  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
-  "mission": {
-    "title": "open-webui: feat: allow login without authentication",
-    "description": "feat: allow login without authentication. Requested by 47+ users.",
-    "type": "feature",
-    "status": "completed",
-    "steps": [
-      {
-        "title": "Check current open-webui deployment",
-        "description": "Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Review open-webui configuration",
-        "description": "Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
Many self hosted programs have an authentication-by-default approach these days."
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Apply the fix for feat: allow login without authentication",
-        "description": "> I think there are probably still quite a few people that want no authentication all together but this is a good step forward.

Not gonna lie, I think I like this solution _better_ than building auth in ourselves, and it's been our stated opinion that we feel auth shouldn't be optional.
```yaml
services:
  open-webui:
    ...
    environment:
      WEBUI_AUTH_TRUSTED_EMAIL_HEADER: 'X-authentik-email'
    networks:
      - traefik-servicenet
    labels:
      traefik.enable: true
      traefik.http.routers.open-webui.middlewares: authentik
      traefik.http.middlewares.open-webui-auth.headers.customrequestheaders.X-authentik-email: "true"
```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Verify the feature works",
-        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feat: allow login without authentication" is functioning correctly."
-      }
-    ],
-    "resolution": {
-      "summary": "> I think there are probably still quite a few people that want no authentication all together but this is a good step forward.

Not gonna lie, I think I like this solution _better_ than building auth in ourselves, and it's been our stated opinion that we feel auth shouldn't be optional.",
-      "codeSnippets": [
-        "services:
  open-webui:
    ...
    environment:
      WEBUI_AUTH_TRUSTED_EMAIL_HEADER: 'X-authentik-email'
    networks:
      - traefik-servicenet
    labels:
      traefik.enable: true
      traefik.http.routers.open-webui.middlewares: authentik
      traefik.http.middlewares.open-webui-auth.headers.customrequestheaders.X-authentik-email: "true"",
-        "&name open-webui:
    container_name: *name
    hostname: *name
    build:
      context: https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui.git#main
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    volumes:
      - /mnt/llm/open-webui/data:/app/backend/data
    depends_on:
      ollama:
        condition: service_started
        restart: false
    links:
      - ollama
    environment:
      OLLAMA_BASE_URL: http://ollama:11434
      SCARF_NO_ANALYTICS: true
      ANONYMIZED_TELEMETRY: false
      DO_NOT_TRACK: true
      ENABLE_SIGNUP: false
      WEBUI_AUTH: false
      RAG_EMBEDDING_MODEL_AUTO_UPDATE: true
      RAG_EMBEDDING_MODEL: "mixedbread-ai/mxbai-embed-large-v1"
    networks:
      - traefik-servicenet
      - default
    labels:
      traefik.enable: true
      traefik.http.routers.open-webui.rule: "Hos",
-        "docker stop open-webui
# (cleared all browser cookies/cache)

rm -rf /mnt/llm/open-webui
docker compose up -d open-webui

v0.1.124 - building the best open-source AI user interface.
https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui
